what_it_does: Node.js CLI entry point for the recollection distill pipeline. It parses a subcommand (run, types, candidates), handles a small --flag argument helper, opens the database, dispatches to pipeline functions, prints JSON results, and exits non-zero when there are distillation failures or errors.
observations: Implements three subcommands: run, types, and candidates.
run reads source exchanges filtered by optional project, session, and limit, then calls runDistill and prints a JSON result including a failures array.
Exits with code 1 if result.failures.length is greater than zero, surfacing the gap explicitly.
types idempotently seeds distill unit types and lists only those whose names match L1_SUMMARY or L2_KINDS.
candidates lists staged principle candidates filtered by status, defaulting to proposed.
Uses a simple flag(name) helper that looks for --name and returns the next argv token.
Wraps database access in try/finally to ensure db.close() is called.
Default behavior prints usage and exits with code 1.
standouts: Explicit consented-write CLI model: the file itself notes that run writes recollection's store as an explicit, consented pass rather than a side effect.
Non-gated vs gated lane language: non-gated records self-assemble into units while gated principle records stage as proposed candidates awaiting ratification.
Default extractor is an in-process stub with no model or network; EXTRACT_URL can point to a served extractor, which fails loudly if unavailable.
Output carries a counted failures[] array and the CLI exits non-zero on any failure.
conventions: Shebang and JSDoc header describing subcommands and behavior.
Switch-based command dispatch in an async main function.
JSON.stringify(..., null, 2) for all command output.
process.exit(1) for errors, usage, and detected gaps.
Simple positional flag parser rather than a full argument-parsing library.
concerns: flag() does not validate that a value follows --name or support --name=value syntax, so malformed flags can silently consume the next token or return undefined.
parseInt for --limit is not validated, so non-numeric values become NaN and are passed through.
No dedicated help or version subcommand; default usage is the only guidance.
The run subcommand exits non-zero after printing JSON, which is intentional but may surprise callers expecting a pure report.
notes: A thin, explicit CLI driver for the distill pipeline that keeps default operation offline and makes failures visible rather than silent.
questions: What is the exact shape of the object returned by runDistill, especially failures[] entries?
How are L1_SUMMARY and L2_KINDS defined and what unit_type vocabulary do they represent?
What do readSourceExchanges and listStagedCandidates return, and how do project/session filters map to source rows?
How is EXTRACT_URL consumed by the distill pipeline, and what does the in-process stub do?
What is the intended ratification flow for staged candidates?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it runs the distill pipeline, lists the seeded unit types, and surfaces staged candidates with explicit failure reporting.
apparent_themes: Explicit consent and controlled write surfaces
Separation of non-gated assembly from gated staging
intention_signals: To give operators a deliberate, observable command-line surface for distilling exchanges, where routine records auto-assemble into units and higher-stakes principles are staged for later ratification, while keeping the default path local and loudly reporting any gaps.
novelty: The framing of a 'beat-4 NON-GATED scaffolding' with a default no-model stub extractor and a CLI that treats its own write operation as an explicit, consented pass rather than an ambient side effect.
contribution: It contributes the command-line dispatch, argument parsing, database lifecycle, JSON output formatting, and failure-escalation logic for the recollection distill pipeline.
summary_for_embedding: A Node.js CLI in company/recollection/src/distill-cli.ts that dispatches run, types, and candidates subcommands for the distill pipeline, prints JSON results, and exits non-zero when distillation failures occur.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an explicit, consented, and observable operator interface for distilling source exchanges into recollection units and staged candidates.
suggested_fields: gated_seam_flag — marker for which operations cross the gated ratification boundary
extractor_url_source — where EXTRACT_URL is read and how it selects the extractor
consent_surface — classification of CLI commands that perform explicit writes
proposed_edge_kinds: cli_dispatches_to — the CLI subcommand routes control to a pipeline function
seeds_vocab — a command initializes a controlled vocabulary before reading it
surface_candidates — a command exposes staged records for human review

symbols.does: Parses command-line flags by finding --name and returning the next argv token.
symbols.description: A minimal helper that scans process.argv for a literal --name flag and returns the following string, or undefined if not found. It is used to read --project, --session, --limit, and --status values for subcommands.

symbols.does: Dispatches CLI subcommands, manages the database lifecycle, prints JSON output, and exits on failures or errors.
symbols.description: An async entry function that opens the database, switches on the provided command to run the distill pipeline, list seeded unit types, or list staged candidates, prints formatted JSON, exits non-zero when failures are present or an error occurs, and ensures the database is closed in a finally block.
